title: Lucy III
content:
I travellï¿½d among unknown men,
In lands beyond the sea;
Nor, England! did I know till then
What love I bore to thee.

ï¿½Tis past, that melancholy dream!
Nor will I quit thy shore
A second time; for still I seem
To love thee more and more.

Among thy mountains did I feel
The joy of my desire;
And she I cherishï¿½d turnï¿½d her wheel
Beside an English fire.

Thy mornings showed, thy nights concealï¿½d,
The bowers where Lucy played;
And thine too is the last green field
That Lucyï¿½s eyes surveyï¿½d.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Wordsworth delivers a powerful performance in "Lucy III"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
